data.title: Goldman Sachs First-Quarter Results Top Views; CEO Expects Market Volatility to Persist
data.description:
Goldman Sachs' first-quarter results surpassed Wall Street's views as equities-trading revenue reached record levels, while the investment bank's chief executive, David Solomon, said that market volatility is expected to continue amid policy uncertainty.

Per-share earnings jumped to $14.12 for the March quarter from $11.58 a year earlier, ahead of the FactSet-polled consensus of $12.32. Revenue rose 6% year over year to $15.06 billion, beating the Street's view for $14.77 billion.

The stock rose 2.1% intraday Monday.

"Our strong results this quarter have demonstrated that in times of great uncertainty, clients turn to Goldman Sachs for execution and insight," Solomon said in a statement. "While we are entering the second quarter with a markedly different operating environment than earlier this year, we remain confident in our ability to continue to support our clients."

President Donald Trump exempted smartphones, computers and semiconductors from his reciprocal tariffs, CNBC reported, citing Customs and Border Protection guidance issued late Friday. However, Trump said in a social media post Sunday that there are no tariff exceptions. Last week, Trump announced a 90-day pause for certain tariffs for non-retaliating countries.

"We are encouraged by the administration's recent actions to pursue a more gradual policy process that allows for considered negotiations with many countries, but how policies will evolve is still unknown," Solomon said on an earnings call, according to a FactSet transcript. "In the meantime, markets will likely continue to be volatile until we have further clarity."

Global banking and markets revenue advanced 10% to $10.71 billion, driven by a 27% surge in equity-trading to $4.19 billion. Investment banking fees fell 8% at $1.91 billion amid "significantly" lower advisory revenue that was partially offset by gains in debt underwriting.

Asset and wealth management revenue fell 3% to $3.68 billion amid declines in equity and debt investments. Total assets under supervision reached $3.173 trillion as of the end of the quarter, up from $2.848 trillion the year before.

"Our economists' expectation for growth in the US has fallen meaningfully from over 2% to 0.5%," Solomon told analysts. "The prospect of a recession has increased, with growing indications that economic activity is slowing down around the world."

data.title: Current Uranium Equity Price Levels "A Good Entry Point", RBC Capital Markets Says
data.description:
Current price levels of uranium equities present a good entry point given a likely bottoming in uranium prices, RBC Capital Markets said in a Friday note as it attended the 2025 World Nuclear Fuel Cycle conference in Montreal.

RBC also expects a potential pickup in contracting activity in the second half and lack of direct trade impacts on uranium.

While the long-term outlook remains positive and unchanged from broader macro volatility, there is a lack of immediate catalysts and broader trade-related macro uncertainty remains a near-term risk, RBC said.

RBC said it continues to be positive on Cameco on Cameco due to its proven production and broad nuclear participation. RBC also mentioned NexGen Energy , which is developing the world's largest uranium project.

"We think the uranium market should see improved activity in H2 given some pent-up demand and prices may have stabilized near current levels, but utilities remain patient and replacement rate contracting may have to wait until 2026," RBC said.

"Additionally, tariff and trade uncertainty has resulted in a significant slow-down in contracting, but uranium remains largely exempt from trade actions and activity could improve if this remains the case going forward," RBC added.

data.title: Italian Billionaire Just Outplayed BlackRock in a $19 Billion Global Port Grab
data.description:
Italian shipping mogul Gianluigi Aponte just pulled off one of the most consequential infrastructure deals of the decade. His family-owned Terminal Investment Ltd. (TiL) is taking over 41 of the 43 ports being sold by Li Ka-shing's CK Hutchison Holdings (CKHUF) in a $19 billion transaction that's making waves far beyond the docks. While the deal was originally framed as a TiL-BlackRock consortium, it's now clear TiL will walk away with the lion's shareleaving BlackRock with just a 51% stake in two ports along the Panama Canal. TiL holds the remaining 49%. That Panama carve-out represents just 4% of the deal's value but sits at the heart of global trade lanes, which explains the geopolitical heat.

This isn't just another port saleit's a chess match. Beijing has pushed back hard, given the U.S. involvement and the Canal's strategic importance. Panama's government isn't thrilled either, accusing CK Hutchison of improprieties (which the company has denied). Still, the buyers have promised to leave current operating models intact and keep the ports open to all shipperslikely a preemptive move to avoid regulatory drama. Notably, CK Hutchison is holding onto its Hong Kong and mainland China ports, which contribute about 12% to its revenue. Shares of CK Hutchison jumped nearly 4% in Hong Kong after the announcement.

Behind the scenes, this is a classic Aponte power play. MSC, already the world's largest container line, will now control even more of the maritime chessboard through TiL's expanded reach across 31 countries. And while BlackRock may still be involved via its infrastructure arm, it's Aponte who now calls the shots. The deal also underscores a quiet but growing shift: family-owned industrial giants are increasingly outmaneuvering institutional investors in global asset grabs. The original April 2 deadline for closing the Panama portion was missed, but sources say the deal should still wrap by year-end. If it does, it'll mark one of the most strategically important port handovers of the decadeand a major win for European shipping muscle over Wall Street capital.

data.title: REG - Insig AI Plc - Order from the Financial Conduct Authority
data.description:
RNS Number : 9636E Insig AI Plc 14 April 2025  

This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

14 April 2025

Insig AI plc

("Insig AI", the "Company" or the "Group")

Order from the Financial Conduct Authority

Insig AI plc (AIM:INSG), the data science and machine learning solutions company, is pleased to announce that it has received a purchase order from the Financial Conduct Authority ("FCA"). The order is for a subscription service licence agreement to access Insig AI's Transparency and Disclosure Index ("TDI") covering UK listed companies. The FCA can access our toolkit that allows users to search, filter, analyse and benchmark company disclosures, which are solely evidence based and traceable to company reports.

The FCA will also have access to ImpactScope's Greenwashing Identifier ("GWI"), an AI and blockchain powered application capable of identifying inconsistencies and unsubstantiated claims in statements and numbers across multiple datasets, including annual reports, ESG reports, earnings call transcripts, social media posts, news articles and carbon offset purchase transaction records. Insig AI is ImpactScope's exclusive agent for sales to international regulators and asset managers. 

In 2023, Insig AI provided the data and software platforms to the FCA's 2023 TechSprint, known as the Global Financial Innovation Network's ("GFIN") Greenwashing TechSprint. The GFIN Greenwashing TechSprint brought together 13 international regulators. The project focused on how technology, including AI and Machine Learning, can enable regulators and supervisors to verify that ESG-related product claims and how technology can help monitor, collate, and identify examples of greenwashing from financial services firms' websites, social media platforms, and other documentation or data which can also be shared across jurisdictions.

Richard Bernstein, Insig AI's Chief Executive commented: "Since collaborating on the FCA's TechSprint, we have been hugely impressed with the FCA's decisiveness in harnessing technology, including AI and Machine Learning. I believe that our curated repository of machine-readable corporate reports and disclosures will assist the FCA. It is not so much the immediate financial implications of this purchase order but rather what we regard as the commercial importance of our work with the FCA. We are proud of this progress."

For further information, please visit www.insig-ai.com or contact: 

Insig AI plc

Richard Bernstein, CEO

richard.bernstein@insg.ai

Zeus (Nominated Adviser & Broker)

David Foreman / James Hornigold

+44 (0) 203 829 5000

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. data.title: Mammoth Energy Completes Infrastructure Subsidiaries Sale, Expands Aircraft Fleet; Shares Jump
data.description:
Mammoth Energy Services surged more than 32% in recent Monday trading after disclosing the sale of three infrastructure subsidiaries to privately held Peak Utility Services Group for a total of $108.7 million.

The company received $98.3 million in cash at closing for its former Higher Power Electrical, 5 Star Electric, and Python Equipment units, with the remaining $10.4 million placed in escrow to cover potential indemnified liabilities and other post-closing adjustments.

Mammoth also said CEO Phil Lancaster will join Peak as an employee on July 1, or earlier if a successor is named.

Following the transaction, Mammoth's cash position rose to about $160 million. The company said it expects improved earnings due to the divestiture and its recent $11.5 million acquisition of eight small passenger aircraft under lease with a commuter airline.

Mammoth also amended its revolving credit facility to permit share repurchases of up to 10 million shares or $50 million, whichever is less.

data.title: REG - Schroder Inc Growth - Compliance with Market Abuse Regulation
data.description:
RNS Number : 9610E Schroder Income Growth Fund PLC 14 April 2025  

14 April 2025

SCHRODER INCOME GROWTH FUND PLC

(the "Company")

Compliance with the Market Abuse Regulation ("MAR")

The Company announces that it is satisfied that all inside information which the Directors and the Company may have in the period leading up to the announcement of its half year results for the six months ended 28 February 2025 has previously been, and will continue to be, notified via a regulatory information service ("RIS"). Therefore, the Company is not prohibited from dealing in its own securities.

The mandatory closed period under MAR will last until the half year results in respect of the six months ended 28 February 2025 are published and is anticipated to end no sooner than 14 May 2025.

If, in the period leading up to the announcement of the half year results, the Directors of the Company come into the possession of any inside information, this will be notified to an RIS before any such transactions are undertaken.

data.title: Asian Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Higher in Monday Trading
data.description:
Asian equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were rising Monday morning, up 1.8% to 1,999.36 on the S&P Asia 50 ADR Index.

From North Asia, the gainers were led by air travel advertisement company AirNet Technology and consumer lending company LexinFintech , which were climbing past 10% each. They were followed by autonomous aerial vehicle technology firm EHang and internet and data center service provider 21Vianet , which gained 9.5% and 4% respectively.

The decliners from North Asia were led by advertising agency 36Kr and semiconductor assembly and testing services company ASE Technology , which lost 2.2% and 0.7%, respectively, followed by telecommunications company KT , which decreased 0.1%.

From South Asia, the gainers were led by tech conglomerate Sea and telecommunications operator Telekomunikasi Indonesia , which added 2.8% and 2.3% respectively. They were followed by IT firms Sify Technologies and Infosys , which were up 1.6% and 1% respectively.

The decliners from South Asia were IT service management company Trident Digital Tech and telecommunications operator PLDT , which were down 1% and 0.2%, respectively.

data.title: Ondas-Developed Protocol Selected to Upgrade Rail Communications Infrastructure
data.description:
Ondas Holdings said Monday the IEEE 802.16t direct peer-to-peer protocol was selected by the Association of American Railroads for head-of-train/end-of-train (HOT/EOT) communications.

The protocol, primarily developed by Ondas, will enhance train telemetry systems for better safety and efficiency, improving the operation of longer trains, according to a company statement.

The IEEE protocol will minimize latency and increase over-the-air communications security for data exchange, it added.

Ondas said the North American rail infrastructure has a significant installed base of legacy systems that will be upgraded to the new Gen4 requirements.

The company said it expects to generate development revenue in H2 to support communications upgrades across the North American rail infrastructure in 2026.

data.title: Robusta coffee rallies amid supply jitters, tariff exemptions
data.description:
World robusta coffee prices rallied on Monday after U.S. exclusions on some tariffs allowed investors to turn their attention back to worries over falling supplies of the instant coffee ingredient.

Data last week showed robusta exports from Brazil, the world's second largest shipper of the bean, tumbled some 84% in March from a year ago. Meanwhile, certified stocks in ICE exchange warehouses (LRC-TOT-VG) are at 1-1/2 month lows.

"The trade is expecting lower Brazilian production this year due to the extended drought last year, the 'off-year' in their biennial production cycle, and disappointing rainfall over the past few months," said broker ADMISI.

At 1514 GMT, robusta coffee futures traded on the ICE exchange, which serve as a global price benchmark, were up 3.2% at $5,209 a metric ton, having lost 1.5% last week.

Arabica coffee futures , used largely in pricier roast and ground blends, rose 2% to $3.6075 per lb, having lost 2.7% last week.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday granted exclusions from steep tariffs on smartphones, computers and some other electronic goods imported largely from China, but then warned on Sunday that levies were still likely.

Investors remain concerned that a trade war could weaken global economic growth and dent demand for consumer goods, but major stock indexes in Europe and Asia nevertheless bounced on Monday thanks to the brief reprieve.

In other soft commodities traded, London cocoa futures fell 1.7% to 6,100 pounds a metric ton, having lost 3% last week, while New York cocoa fell 1.4% to $8,323 a metric ton, having lost 1% last week.

Cocoa investors continue to fear a downturn in demand for the chocolate ingredient thanks to historically high prices.

Dealers said first quarter cocoa grind data, a measure of demand, will likely show steep falls of 5-7% later this week.

Supplies remain tight, however. Farmers in top producer Ivory Coast warned on Monday of mid-crop supply shortages due to dry weather.

Raw sugar ​​was down 0.6% at 78.89 cents per lb, having hit a 1-1/2 month low earlier, while white sugar fell 0.7% to $519.30 a ton.

data.title: REG - CLS Holdings PLC - Publication of 2024 Annual Report & Notice of AGM
data.description:
RNS Number : 9611E CLS Holdings PLC 14 April 2025  

CLS Holdings plc

("CLS" or the "Company")

Publication of 2024 Annual Report and Accounts and Notice of Annual General Meeting

Further to the publication of our audited financial results on 1 April 2025, CLS announces that the following documents were posted or otherwise made available to shareholders of the Company today:

  • Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2024;
  • Notice of Annual General Meeting to be held on 16 May 2025; and
  • Form of Proxy for the 2025 Annual General Meeting.

In accordance with DTR 6.3.5(1A), the unedited full text of the regulated information required to be made public under DTR 4.1 is contained within the 2024 Annual Report which, pursuant to UKLR 6.4.1R, will be uploaded shortly to the National Storage Mechanism and will be available on the Company's website www.clsholdings.com.

The 2025 Notice of Annual General Meeting will also be available shortly on the National Storage Mechanism and the CLS website at www.clsholdings.com.

-ENDS-

data.title: Pfizer Halts Obesity-Drug Development. What It Means for Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk.
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Pfizer will discontinue the development of an orally administered experimental treatment for weight management after one patient fell ill, the drugmaker said in a statement Monday.

Shares fell in premarket trading on the news, but then rebounded to open 0.7% higher at $22.05. It had traded about 2% higher prior to the announcement.

The drug, danuglipron, is similar to treatments by Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk. Investors had hoped that it could eventually rival blockbuster drugs Zepbound and Wegovy, which are administered via injections.

One person who was treated with the GLP-1 drug experienced a potential drug-induced liver injury, which resolved after discontinuation of the treatment, Pfizer said.

Chief Scientific Officer Chris Boshoff said the discontinuation of danuglipron was disappointing but that cardiovascular and metabolic diseases remain important areas of unmet medical need.

"This is a blow to [Pfizer's] attempt to become a meaningful obesity player, with them now arguably falling behind Roche and other competitors," Bernstein analysts led by Courtney Breen said. "We now expect Pfizer to aggressively engage in M&A/licensing activities to fill-out their pipeline."

Although Pfizer plans to continue developing another oral small-molecule weight-loss drug currently in mid-stage clinical trials, with an expected readout early in 2026, investors will undoubtedly be disappointed. Pfizer declined to comment further on its obesity pipeline.

The stock is down 17% so far this year through Friday's close. It has slid 64% from highs reached in December 2021, during the Covid-19 pandemic.

GLP-1 drugs work by controlling blood sugar levels and appetite. So far, there are no weight-loss medication in pill form approved by the Food and Drug Administration, which is another reason why investors had been hopeful about Pfizer's danuglipron.

An oral weight-loss drug would likely benefit from higher demand from consumers compared with injectables. It would also make distribution and storing of these drugs easier as, unlike injectables, pills don't require cold storage.

Shares of rivals Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk's American-listed shares rose 0.8% and 1.7%, respectively, after Pfizer's announcement. Viking Therapeutics, which has an experimental obesity drug in clinical trials, rallied 13%.

Lilly, the largest pharmaceutical company by market value, also has an oral obesity candidate in development, orforglipron. "Given Pfizer's commentary on the liver profile, we don't see this as an increased risk for orforglipron," Breen said. "Instead, we believe this now sets LLY up with more time."

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Monday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. Stocks Up as Tariff Reprieve Powers Tech Rally

U.S. stocks rose, with the Nasdaq Composite leading the way. The Trump administration's latest zigzag on trade policy boosted hardware companies such as Apple and Dell, and chip stocks like Nvidia and Micron.

OPEC Cuts Oil Demand Outlook Amid U.S. Tariff Hit

The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries cut its forecast for oil demand growth just days after a surprise decision to boost output, citing the impact of U.S. tariffs on the global economy and crude consumption.

Shifting Signals on Tech Tariffs Fuel Fresh Trade Uncertainty

Various comments from the Trump administration over the weekend regarding tariffs on consumer electronics hint at another chaotic week on Wall Street and in Washington.

China's Exports Surge as Orders Front-Loaded Before Tariffs

China's exports surged in March as shipments were likely front-loaded before President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, but they are expected to falter in the coming months.

German Exports to U.S. Hit Highest in Two Decades

The uptick could be short-lived given the Trump administration's raising of tariff barriers on imports from Europe.

At China's Wholesale Hub, U.S. Orders Have Suddenly Halted. One Example: Socks.

Chinese sellers remain confident despite higher tariffs, saying it no longer makes sense to sell everyday goods to Americans.

ECB to Cut Rates Again as Tariffs Darken Growth Outlook

The case for a cut has been bolstered by the surprising strength of the euro in the wake of the April 2 tariff announcements.

U.S. Allies Are Sitting Out Trump's Trade War With China

The imposition of new tariffs on nearly every country has added to concerns in Europe and Asia about relying on America.

Trump's Trade War Is Threatening to Derail the Office-Market Recovery

Leasing by businesses hit a recent high in the first quarter, but economic uncertainty is prompting a pause in some dealmaking.

Tariff Upheaval Threatens Private Equity's Exit Recovery Hopes

U.S. private-equity exits and their total value rose in the first quarter compared with a year ago, but recent market volatility puts that momentum at risk.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Monday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Wall Street's Trading Revenue Powered by Tariff Uncertainty

Goldman Sachs is the latest big bank to report that increased trading fueled its quarterly profit.

CME Group, S&P Group Agree to Sell OSTTRA to KKR in $3.1 Billion Deal

CME Group and S&P Global have agreed to sell OSTTRA to investment funds managed by investment firm KKR for an enterprise value of $3.1 billion.

Morgan Stanley Collects $2.3 Billion for Private Equity Co-Investments

The investment bank's asset management arm sees robust demand for co-investment capital, particularly to co-underwrite deals.

BNP Paribas Downgrades Projections for AXA Investment Managers Deal

The lender expects a higher capital impact and lower return on investment than initially projected for its acquisition of AXA Investment Managers, after the ECB clarified its regulatory stance.

MPS Gets Italy's Green Light for Mediobanca Deal as Top Shareholders Signal Support

Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena launched a surprise $14 billion-takeover offer for its peer in January.

Temasek-Backed Private-Equity Firm Raises Over $600 Mln in Latest Funding Round

Private-equity firm ABC Impact has raised over $600.0 million in its latest funding round, drawing interest from investors, including a multilateral development bank and a sovereign-wealth fund.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Macquarie Group, Netwealth and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

7 Market Pros on Wall Street's Wild Month

President Trump's tariff policy has set off a wave of selling in the stock and bond markets. These savvy investors are buying, too.

The U.S. Bond Market Just Had One of Its Worst Selloffs. How a Haven Turned So Volatile.

The 30-year yield gained 0.482 percentage points, its largest one-week gain since 1987, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

The Simple Explanation for This Week's Treasury Market Mayhem

While leveraged trades blowing up may have played a small role, traders' search for a boogeyman ignores the obvious.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Monday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Crude Futures Pull Back From Early Highs

Oil futures gained, but are off the morning's highs, as demand concerns remain intact, with OPEC cutting its growth outlook for this year by 150,000 barrels a day.

OPEC Cuts Oil Demand Outlook Amid U.S. Tariff Hit

The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries cut its forecast for oil demand growth just days after a surprise decision to boost output, citing the impact of U.S. tariffs on the global economy and crude consumption.

BP Discovers Oil at U.S. Far South Prospect in Gulf of Mexico

Initial data shows high-quality reservoirs and a potentially commercial volume of hydrocarbons, the company said.

Trump Has a $350 Billion Deal for Europe: Buy Our Energy

The continent has sharply increased its U.S. purchases in recent years, with little appetite to buy more.

Nuclear Power Is Back. And This Time, AI Can Help Manage the Reactors.

Argonne National Lab has an AI-based tool that can help design and operate nuclear reactors-at a time when AI itself is feeding a power frenzy.

BP Expects First-Quarter Upstream Production to Fall

The company anticipates lower upstream production than the previous quarter due to already announced divestments in Egypt and Trinidad.

Trump Promised a Golden Age for Oil and Gas, but Tariffs Undermine His Pledge

Fractured trade relations and a potential global recession are poised to reduce international sales and domestic output of fossil fuels.

AI Boom to Fuel Surge in Data Center Energy Needs, IEA Says

The global push for artificial intelligence is poised to fuel a sharp rise in electricity demand, with consumption from data centers expected to more than double by the end of the decade, according to the International Energy Agency.

Equinor Creates New Power Business to Cater to Data-Center Demand

The company said growing demand for power as well as the expansion of AI and data centers drove the move.

U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Rise for Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories rose for a second consecutive week, climbing 2.6 million barrels, amid an increase in net imports.
